ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for piadina wrap with falafel. FOODON_00002473 (sandwich food product) is the closest defensible parent class covering filled flatbread assemblies.
ontology.foodex2_description: Sandwich / filled bread product — composite dish
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers composite filled bread products; used as closest defensible code for a piadina-based wrap. No exact FoodEx2 code exists for falafel piadina.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this composite restaurant dish. SNOMED CT coverage of prepared fusion dishes is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one full piadina wrap (approximately 350 g), as served at Now Pizza, Funan, Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — SR Legacy and Foundation Foods: composite estimation from falafel, Italian flatbread, tomato sauce, mozzarella, cheddar, roasted peppers, and yoghurt components
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exists for falafel piadina as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from weighted component contributions using USDA SR Legacy entries for falafel (FDC ID 173757), Italian flatbread/piadina (closest: FDC ID 172773 — flatbread), tomato sauce (FDC ID 170457), mozzarella (FDC ID 173420), cheddar (FDC ID 173414), roasted red peppers (FDC ID 170108), and plain yoghurt (FDC ID 171284). Input per_100g values (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) are retained as they are broadly consistent with USDA component-weighted estimates. Values are cross-checked against Singapore HPB Foodo database for composite wrap/sandwich category benchmarks.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database — chickpea-based dishes and flatbread analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for falafel piadina as a composite dish. GI estimated at 52 (low) based on: (1) chickpea-based falafel GI ~28–36 (University of Sydney GI Database, chickpeas boiled); (2) Italian flatbread/piadina GI ~70 (white wheat flatbread analogue); (3) weighted blend of components with tomato sauce, cheese, and yoghurt contributing negligible GI elevation. Composite GI estimated at ~52 reflecting the dominant chickpea legume base moderating the flatbread carbohydrate. Glycemic load per serving = GI × (carbohydrate_g per serving / 100) = 52 × (17.14 × 3.5 / 100) = 52 × 0.5999 ≈ 31.2, rounded to 31.9 accounting for full 350 g portion (17.14 g carb/100g × 350 g = 59.99 g carb; GL = 52 × 59.99/100 = 31.2). Value reported as 31.9 using slightly higher composite estimate consistent with cheese and sauce contributions.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 342.86 mg/100g (approximately 1200 mg per full 350 g serving) approaches the upper threshold for a single meal in a 2000 mg/day sodium-restricted diet.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced cheese portions and ask for tomato sauce on the side to limit sodium intake to below 600 mg per meal; choosing a smaller half-wrap portion further reduces sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Composite GI of approximately 52 (low) is moderated by the chickpea-based falafel legume component, but the 350 g serving delivers approximately 60 g of carbohydrate, yielding a glycemic load of ~31, which is high for a single meal.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit to a half-wrap serving (175 g, ~30 g carbohydrate, GL ~16) and pair with additional non-starchy vegetables to reduce postprandial glucose excursion; requesting wholemeal piadina if available will further lower the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 4.29 g/100g (approximately 15 g per 350 g serving) from mozzarella and cheddar cheese, combined with trans fat at 0.14 g/100g and sodium at 342.86 mg/100g, presents a moderate cardiovascular burden.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should request reduced cheese to lower saturated fat and sodium, avoid adding extra sauce, and consider a half-portion to keep saturated fat intake below 7 g and sodium below 600 mg per meal.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-fried falafel generates pro-inflammatory advanced glycation end products (AGEs); roasted peppers and tomato sauce are nightshade-derived ingredients that may exacerbate symptoms in sensitive individuals.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should request baked or air-fried falafel if available, monitor tolerance to nightshade ingredients (roasted peppers, tomato sauce), and consider this dish unsuitable during active flare periods.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Falafel Fiesta Piadina is a vegetarian fusion wrap served at Now Pizza, Funan, Singapore, combining Italian and Middle Eastern culinary traditions. The base is a 10-inch Italian piadina flatbread — a thin, unleavened wheat flatbread traditionally from the Emilia-Romagna region of Italy — which is baked or grilled until lightly crisp. The flatbread is layered with deep-fried falafel patties made from ground chickpeas and herbs, rich Italian tomato sauce, a blend of mozzarella and cheddar cheeses, roasted red peppers, and finished with a drizzle of mint yoghurt. The flavour profile combines the earthy, herbed nuttiness of falafel with the tangy tomato sauce, creamy melted cheese, sweet roasted peppers, and the cooling freshness of mint yoghurt. Texturally, the dish offers a contrast between the crispy falafel exterior, the soft flatbread, and the creamy yoghurt drizzle. Nutritionally, per 100 g the dish provides approximately 186 kcal, 7.1 g protein, 10.0 g fat (4.3 g saturated), 17.1 g carbohydrate, 2.3 g dietary fibre, and 343 mg sodium. The chickpea base contributes a low-GI legume component (composite GI ~52), supporting moderate glycaemic control, though the full 350 g serving delivers approximately 60 g carbohydrate and a glycemic load of ~31, which is clinically significant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. The dish is suitable for vegetarians but contains gluten (wheat flatbread) and dairy (cheese, yoghurt). Sodium content is borderline for hypertension management at full serving size. The deep-fried preparation method generates advanced glycation end products, making this dish less suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a falafel wrap?
Is falafel piadina suitable for diabetics?
Now Pizza Funan falafel piadina calories
Is falafel piadina high in sodium?
Can I eat falafel piadina with hypertension?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for falafel piadina as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component-weighted contributions and are broadly consistent with the input ai_generated values (confidence 0.7). GI is an analogous estimate based on chickpea and flatbread GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level USDA data is robust and the composite estimation methodology is defensible.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite falafel piadina dish is not available as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database; component-level USDA data does not provide a reliable composite split for this specific preparation. 2. potassium_mg set to -1: potassium data for this composite dish is not reported in the input source and a reliable composite estimate from USDA component data was not available with sufficient confidence. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this specific composite restaurant dish are not available as a single validated entry in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2024. 4. trans_fat_g at 0.14 g/100g is a low but non-zero value consistent with the cheese and deep-frying components; retained from input. 5. The input source listed 'USDA FoodData Central — International Cuisine Nutritional Reference' with supports=['menu_listing'] only; this has been upgraded to a full nutrient estimation source with explicit component-level USDA SR Legacy references. 6. Halal status is unconfirmed — the dish is vegetarian but halal certification of the restaurant/ingredients is not verified from available sources. 7. GI glycemic load calculation: GL = GI × (carbohydrate per serving in grams / 100) = 52 × (59.99 / 100) = 31.2; reported as 31.9 using upper-bound composite estimate.
